understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously for WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece must be selected for movement commands to apply. CLICK appears to be a move command: clicking a destination tile can relocate the selected piece to that tile if the destination is valid/reachable (attempts on invalid, blocked, or unreachable tiles result in no movement). MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move the currently selected piece, but movement is prevented by walls/obstacles (blocked moves do nothing). I did not observe any successful pushing; when movement was blocked it simply failed rather than pushing something out of the way.

orient_history: Hypothesis: CLICK changes which selectable is controlled; movement acts in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps and is blocked by collidable walls. Need to position selectables onto all goal tiles to win.

CLICK appears to relocate/place the currently selected selectable to the clicked position (movement is otherwise blocked by walls). Winning likely requires getting a selectable overlapping the goal region.

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking a selectable, and/or attempts to reposition the currently selected piece; since the current selected piece is immobile, switching to the other selectable at [9,21] is necessary to make progress toward occupying the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable changes the active/selected piece; movement is via MOVE_* but may only be possible for one of the pieces depending on its surrounding tiles. Need to test the other selectable’s possible moves.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a selectable piece (it doesn’t actually move pieces). Movement is done via MOVE_*; some pieces may be trapped while another has an exit. Next we should select the other piece and re-check its available moves.

New hypothesis (discarding the old): arrow MOVE actions may not be for moving the piece through space here; instead they may change which selectable is currently selected (cycle focus), or otherwise change state even when physical movement is blocked. Progress likely requires switching selection and then using the correct interaction pattern to occupy the goal(s).

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently controlled (engine reports it as the selected piece 'moving' to the clicked selectable’s position). Since the currently selected piece is immobilized, I should select the other piece to see if it has a path toward the goal area.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently controlled (the 'player'), rather than moving a piece to arbitrary coordinates; MOVE_* then attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by walls.

Clicks can move the currently-selected piece to the clicked selectable’s position (teleport/swap-like). If one selectable is stuck, I should click to move to the other and try navigating from there toward the goal.

Clicks seem to move the selected piece only when the clicked cell is in the same row/column as the piece (earlier successful clicks stayed on y=21). It may be possible to click onto a goal tile in the same row even if walls lie between.

CLICK appears to switch/select a selectable (no effect on empty/goal tiles). MOVE_* moves the currently selected 3x3 piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked by walls. To make progress, we should select the other piece and see if it has any available moves.

New hypothesis: CLICK is not the main way to progress; instead, pieces must be navigated via MOVE_* in open corridors, but the currently-selected piece appears trapped, so a movement attempt may be needed to confirm whether any hidden mechanic (like swapping selection, pushing, or breaking out) triggers on MOVE when blocked.

CLICK likely changes selection when clicking a selectable; clicking empty usually does nothing. New hypothesis: clicking directly on the goal area may place/teleport the currently selected selectable onto the goal (since both pieces are trapped by walls and cannot be navigated there by MOVE).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts the currently selected 3x3 piece by 3 pixels (one cell) but it is boxed in by walls. CLICK attempts to reposition the selected piece to the clicked pixel location only if the destination is valid/aligned (likely on the 3-pixel grid) and not blocked; prior failed CLICKs may have been due to misalignment (e.g., [35,17]) or landing on an invalid/blocked spot. To win we likely need to place a selectable onto the goal area.

Hypothesis: CLICK only works if the target point is a valid landing point (likely within a non-wall area and possibly needing grid alignment). Earlier failed goal-clicks may have been outside the goal rectangle or on wall pixels; try a grid-aligned point that lies within the goal object’s 5x5 pixels.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects (activates) the selectable at the clicked position; MOVE_* then attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels), but can be blocked by walls. To make progress, we should switch to the other selectable and test its mobility/path toward the goal.

CLICK likely repositions the selected piece to a clicked reachable/open location (or switches selection to an existing selectable at that location). Since MOVE is fully blocked from [18,21], progress requires switching/repositioning via CLICK to the other pocket at [12,18] to explore alternate movement options toward the goal.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is only for selecting/teleporting between a limited set of anchor positions, while MOVE_* is the primary way to traverse within the walkable area and may also change state (e.g., switch which selectable is active or exit a cramped pocket) even when the UI says 'blocked'. I need to probe with movement to discover a valid route/state change.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (and may appear as the selected piece ‘moving’ to the clicked selectable’s location). To make progress, I should select the other piece and check if it has different movement options or can interact differently with the goal area.

CLICK seems to switch control to (or snap selection onto) the selectable at the clicked position. Arrow moves then move the currently selected selectable in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps, subject to wall collisions.

CLICK likely switches which selectable is active (appearing as a 'move' in the log), while MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels) but is blocked by walls. To make progress we must select the other piece and test whether it has a path toward the goal area.

Clicks appear to sometimes relocate the selected piece when the target square is reachable/valid; far clicks were blocked. Since MOVE is impossible from this pocket, the next hypothesis is that CLICKing adjacent wall tiles may toggle/remove them (or otherwise open a passage) even when movement is blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece to the clicked tile if it is a valid open tile (and swaps if another selectable occupies it); arrow moves only work when adjacent cell is not a wall. We should CLICK to a known-valid alternate tile to regain mobility/line-of-sight options.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): arrow-key MOVE actions attempt to shift the selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels) but collision with surrounding wall tiles can fully lock a piece in place; CLICK likely changes selection or relocates only when a valid reachable/allowed target exists. Right now the selected piece appears completely enclosed, so a MOVE action should confirm whether any direction is actually passable despite the blocked flags.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is controlled (the reported 'movement' on CLICK is actually the selected piece changing). Arrow MOVEs translate the selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) unless blocked by a wall. To make progress, I need to switch selection and see if the other piece has any available movement toward the goal area.

Working hypothesis: MOVE_* is local grid movement and can be blocked by adjacent walls; CLICK can directly reposition (teleport) the selected selectable to the clicked location if it is a valid empty/goal tile (and fails if clicking a wall/invalid). Best path with low remaining steps is to CLICK onto the goal region.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only shifts by 1 cell (3 px) but is prevented by adjacent collidable walls. CLICK can relocate the selected piece to certain valid tiles, notably the position of the other selectable (possibly a swap/teleport/select mechanic). To reach the goal, I may need to reposition/alternate using the second selectable as an intermediate before attempting a CLICK to the goal again.

Hypothesis: CLICK teleports the selected piece to the clicked location only if the target is a valid, non-wall placement aligned to the 3-pixel grid. The goal object spans pixels [35..39]x[17..21], so a grid-aligned point inside it like [36,18] may be a valid teleport target (unlike [35,17]).

CLICK appears to change which selectable is currently controlled (the 'player'), rather than moving through walls. Since the currently selected piece is immobile, we should switch selection to the other piece and see if it has any available movement or interaction options.

New hypothesis (based only on observed outcomes): MOVE_* attempts a 1-cell translation and is blocked by walls; CLICK does not freely move through walls and may effectively switch which selectable is active when clicking certain tiles/objects. Since CLICK spamming failed, try a MOVE action to see if it changes selection/state despite being blocked for movement.

Clicks sometimes move the selected piece directly to the clicked position if a valid path/slot exists; otherwise nothing happens. Movement via arrows is grid-based but currently fully blocked. Win requires a selectable to occupy the goal area.

Based on observed behavior, CLICK attempts to move the currently selected piece to the clicked cell if a valid path/placement exists; otherwise it has no effect. The goal region appears separated by walls from the selectables’ current enclosure, and with 0 steps remaining we cannot reposition further to satisfy the win condition.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Win each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' is occupied by a selectable piece at the same time (if there are multiple '+' tiles, all must be covered simultaneously for WIN).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A piece must be selected for movement commands to apply. CLICK appears to be a move command: clicking a destination tile can relocate the selected piece to that tile if the destination is valid/reachable (attempts on invalid, blocked, or unreachable tiles result in no movement). MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT move the currently selected piece, but movement is prevented by walls/obstacles (blocked moves do nothing). I did not observe any successful pushing; when movement was blocked it simply failed rather than pushing something out of the way.
